<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the current state of all three files. They all have hardcoded DSN values and no environment setting. I need to update them to:
1. Use `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` for the DSN (using NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix for client-side access)
2. Add an `environment` property that reads from an env var like `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT` or similar

For the client-side file, I need to use `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefixed env vars. For server/edge, I could use either, but for consistency, I'll use `NEXT_PUBLIC_` so they all work the same way.

Actually, for server-side configs, I could use non-prefixed env vars, but since the DSN will be the same across all three files, it makes sense to use `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` for all of them.

Let me update all three files.</details></think>

---

All three files have been updated to use environment variables. You'll need to add these env vars to your `.env` file(s):

```
NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=https://070e7da893633d50c5c559652ff2d993@o4510765768310784.ingest.de.sentry.io/4510777732366416
NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT=development
```

The `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix is used so the variables are available both on the server and client side. Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT` to values like `development`, `staging`, or `production` depending on your deployment environment.

                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2358,
                  "content": "### Maintenance Mode",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2359,
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2360,
                  "content": "Set &#96;MAINTENANCE_MODE=true&#96; in &#96;.env.local&#96; to show a maintenance page. When enabled:"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2361,
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2362,
                  "content": "- All page routes (&#96;/login&#96;, &#96;/agents&#96;, etc.) redirect to &#96;/&#96;",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2363,
                  "content": "- &#96;/&#96; displays the maintenance page (no auth required)",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2402,
                  "content": "  - For simulation runs, a special &#96;notFoundHeader&#96; is used that shows empty header and navigates back to &#96;/simulations&#96; (main page) instead of the specific simulation",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2403,
                  "content": "- **Wait for token**: In &#96;useEffect&#96; hooks, return early if &#96;backendAccessToken&#96; is not yet available; include it in the dependency array",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2404,
                  "content": "- **ngrok header**: &#96;\"ngrok-skip-browser-warning\": \"true\"&#96; header is included automatically by API utilities",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2405,
                  "content": "- **Backend URL check**: API utilities will throw an error if &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL&#96; is not set"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2406,
                  "content": "- **Date formatting**: API returns ISO dates; use &#96;toLocaleString()&#96; for display",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2407,
                  "content": "- **UTC timestamps**: Backend returns timestamps in UTC without timezone indicator (e.g., &#96;\"2026-01-18 10:00:00\"&#96;). When parsing for relative time calculations, append &#96;\"Z\"&#96; to explicitly mark as UTC: &#96;new Date(dateString.replace(\" \", \"T\") + \"Z\")&#96;. Without this, JavaScript interprets the timestamp as local time, causing incorrect relative times (e.g., \"5 hours ago\" instead of \"just now\" for users in IST)",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2408,
                  "content": "- **Multiple date formats**: When creating optimistic UI updates (e.g., adding a pending run to a table), use &#96;new Date().toISOString()&#96; which produces &#96;\"2026-01-18T09:30:00.000Z\"&#96;. The &#96;formatRelativeTime&#96; helper in &#96;TestsTabContent.tsx&#96; handles both formats - check if the string already has a timezone indicator before appending \"Z\" to avoid invalid dates like &#96;\"...ZZ\"&#96; which produce NaN",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2647,
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2648,
                  "content": "### Vercel Deployment",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2649,
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2650,
                  "content": "- **&#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_*&#96; vars are build-time**: These environment variables are embedded into client-side JavaScript at build time, not runtime. Server-side code (API routes) reads them at runtime."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2651,
                  "content": "- **Changing env vars requires rebuild**: After adding/changing &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_*&#96; vars in Vercel, you must redeploy **without build cache** to rebuild client JS with new values."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2652,
                  "content": "- **Environment selection**: Vercel has Production, Preview, and Development environments. Set env vars for the correct environment (or \"All\")."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2653,
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2654,
                  "content": "### Analytics",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2655,
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2656,
                  "content": "- **Vercel Analytics**: Enabled via &#96;@vercel/analytics/next&#96; - automatically tracks page views on Vercel deployments",
                  "isContextLine": true
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2657,
                  "content": "- **Google Analytics**: Enabled via &#96;@next/third-parties/google&#96; - only loads when &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID&#96; is set"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 2658,
                  "content": "  - Both are added in the root layout (&#96;src/app/layout.tsx&#96;)",
                  "isContextLine": true

                  "content": "  - Google Analytics component conditionally renders based on the environment variable"
